steps.n: Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to make it mean 'he saw' in the past
Added ت with fatha at the end to show 'you' did the action, and added sukun before it for pronunciation

steps.m: have you seen? (asking about ٱلَّذِى يُكَذِّبُ - the one who denies)
steps.n: Added أَ at beginning to make it a question. This word is asking about the person mentioned next who denies the religion.
This is a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence, like 'who' or 'which' in English. It doesn't come from a root.
Adding ال makes it more specific, like changing 'who' to 'the one who'

steps.m: the one who/that which (subject of يُكَذِّبُ)
steps.n: Final spelling adjustments: Changed ا to ٱ, added shadda on ل, added vowel marks, and changed ي to ى. This word connects to يُكَذِّبُ (denies/rejects) in the ayah, showing who is doing the denying.

steps.m: to deny/reject something that is true

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this isn't just simple denial - it's intense or repeated rejection
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start, changing middle vowels, and adding ُ at end. This word describes what the person (الَّذِى) does.

steps.m: to follow a way or system

steps.n: Adding kasra and connecting letters creates a noun meaning 'religion' or 'system of life'
Adding ال makes it specific and doubles the د letter

steps.m: with/by the religion (object being denied in يُكَذِّبُ)
steps.n: Adding بِ means 'with' or 'by'. The ِ at the end shows it's connected to the verb يُكَذِّبُ (denies/rejects). The hamza on the ال becomes silent.
This is a demonstrative particle that points to something
Added لِ and كَ to make 'this' become 'that' - like pointing with your finger to something far away instead of close by

steps.m: so that (referring to what follows in يَدُعُّ)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result or consequence of something. This word connects to the following verb يَدُعُّ (pushes away/repels) to explain who does that action.
This is a relative pronoun that connects parts of a sentence
Adding ال makes it specifically point to someone or something that will be described

steps.m: the one who (connects to يَدُعُّ - pushes away)
steps.n: Final spelling adjustments: 1) The hamza on the alif becomes a waṣla mark (ٱ), 2) The ل gets a shadda (ّ), 3) The ي becomes ى. This word connects to يَدُعُّ (pushes away) to tell us who does the pushing.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled ع (shown by ّ) makes the pushing action stronger

steps.m: he pushes/shoves very roughly (subject of فَذٰلِكَ ٱلَّذِى)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels to ُ. This word describes what the person mentioned in فَذٰلِكَ ٱلَّذِى (that is the one who) does.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: one who has lost a parent
steps.n: Changes from a verb about lacking something to a noun meaning someone who lost a parent

steps.m: the one who has lost a parent
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to a specific orphan (the one being pushed away in this verse)

steps.m: the orphan (being pushed away by يَدُعُّ)
steps.n: The fatha at the end shows this orphan is being pushed away (object of يَدُعُّ - he pushes away)
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَحُضُّ (urges/encourages) that comes after it. It's a basic word used to express negation.
The و adds the meaning of 'and' to connect this negation with what came before. Together with يَحُضُّ it means 'and does not urge'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense verb
Changed middle vowel to damma to show it's a natural characteristic
Adding يَ (ya) at the start changes it from past tense (he was) to present tense (he is/does). Like changing 'ran' to 'runs'
This is a basic word used to connect other words by showing relationships like 'upon' or 'on'. Here it connects 'urging' (يَحُضُّ) with 'food' (طَعَامِ)
Added a special mark (ٰ) that shows this is a long 'aa' sound at the end. This doesn't change the meaning - it just helps with pronunciation.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning the thing that is provided as food
The kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is possessed by المِسْكِينِ (the needy person) who comes after it

steps.m: to be still or have no movement

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha to make basic past tense verb
Added مِ at start and changed vowels to show someone affected by stillness/lack of movement (metaphorically: someone who can't act due to poverty)
Added ال to make it specific and ِ to show it's the object of feeding in طَعَامِ (feeding of the poor person)
Connected the letters with vowel marks to form the basic word. The fatha and sukun create the standard pronunciation.
Added فَ to show this is a result of something mentioned before
Added tanween to show this is the subject of the sentence and connects to 'for the praying ones' (لِّلْمُصَلِّينَ) that follows it

steps.m: to pray or connect with something
steps.n: The word becomes the noun 'prayer'
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show strong action. The و changes to ى at the end.
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show who does the action
Added ين to make it plural, showing many people who pray
Added لِلْ at start to show 'for/to' (this word comes after وَيْلٌ meaning 'woe')
Added shadda on ل for emphasis and َ at end because it follows وَيْلٌ (woe)
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one person
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ letter to make it mean 'those who'. This word describes the people who are سَاهُونَ (heedless) of their prayers.

steps.m: they (who are عَن صَلَاتِهِمْ سَاهُونَ - heedless of their prayer)
steps.n: This is a personal pronoun that refers to a group of males or a mixed group. In this ayah, it refers to those who are heedless of their prayers.
This is a preposition that shows distance or separation. In this ayah, it shows people being distant or heedless from their prayers.
The root letters ص-ل-و become صَلَا when making it into a noun meaning 'prayer'. The last letter و changes to ا because it's weak and comes at the end of the word.
Added taa marbouta (ة) at the end to make it feminine and specify a single instance of prayer
Added هِمْ with kasra connector to show possession. This prayer belongs to those described as being heedless/negligent in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the past tense verb
Changes from verb (being heedless) to noun (person who is heedless)
This is the basic form meaning 'who'. It's used to connect descriptions to people or things, like saying 'the one who' in English.
Added ال at the beginning and doubled the ذ with a shadda to make it specific. This changes it from just 'who' to 'the one who'.

steps.m: those who (are showing off in يُرَآءُونَ)
steps.n: Added ن at the end to make it plural. This changes it from 'the one who' to 'those who'. This word connects to يُرَآءُونَ (they show off) in the ayah, describing the people who are showing off.
This is a basic pronoun that refers to a group of people (all male or mixed gender). In this ayah, it's connected to the verb يُرَآءُونَ (they show off).
Added damma (ُ) on ه and sukun (ْ) on م to show the correct pronunciation.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Adding alif makes it mean doing the action with/towards others - here showing off to be seen
Adding يُ at the start and changing the ending to ي makes it 'he shows off' in present tense instead of past

steps.m: they show off (to be seen by others)
steps.n: Adding ون at the end makes it plural ('they' instead of 'he') - used when people do things to show off to others
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Adding ي at the start and changing the vowels makes it present tense - 'is withholding' instead of 'withheld'
Adding ون shows that multiple people (they) are doing the action
Adding و at the start means 'and'
Added ما at the start and changed the root letters to make a noun meaning small helpful items that people share with neighbors

steps.m: the useful items that help others (object of يَمْنَعُونَ)
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific and ن at the end. This word is what is being withheld in the phrase 'they withhold the small acts of kindness'
